intro: Iconify and LottieFiles diverge first on pricing model: Iconify uses free pricing with a free tier, LottieFiles uses freemium pricing with a free tier. Everything else flows from that commercial choice. Iconify is built for projects that need access to many icon sets without bundling each library individually and is not a fit for tiny static sites or teams that only ever use one curated icon set. LottieFiles is built for design and product teams integrating lottie animations across web and mobile apps and is not a fit for solo designers needing only a lightweight web editor or teams avoiding subscription tools. On the scoring side, Iconify rates 5/5 for developer experience and 5/5 for transparency; LottieFiles rates 5/5 and 3/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Iconify you accept that runtime lookup adds a small performance cost, while LottieFiles comes with the reality that pricing has crept up for team plans. Neither side is free.
quick_take: Iconify is for projects that need access to many icon sets without bundling each library individually; LottieFiles is for design and product teams integrating lottie animations across web and mobile.
verdict_a: Choose Iconify if you are projects that need access to many icon sets without bundling each library individually and a free pricing model fits how you buy.
verdict_b: Choose LottieFiles if you are design and product teams integrating lottie animations across web and mobile apps and a freemium pricing model fits how you buy.

faq.a: Iconify uses a free model, while LottieFiles uses a freemium model, and both publish a free tier. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Iconify to LottieFiles?
faq.a: Iconify has low lock-in (open-source icon framework); LottieFiles has low lock-in (lottie json open format). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Iconify; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.
Both Iconify and LottieFiles score 5/5 on developer experience in our data, so DX isn't the tiebreaker here. Decide on fit, pricing, or lock-in instead.

faq.q: Is LottieFiles a good alternative to Iconify?
faq.a: LottieFiles is built for design and product teams integrating lottie animations across web and mobile apps and explicitly not for solo designers needing only a lightweight web editor or teams avoiding subscription tools, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Iconify for projects that need access to many icon sets without bundling each library individually, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
